east-village.note: Walk to Essex St for the J/Z straight to Sutphin Blvd, then the $8.75 AirTrain; slow but only one transfer — a car runs ~$70-100.

lower-east-side.note: One of the easier downtown JFK rides: J/Z from Delancey-Essex runs direct to Sutphin Blvd-Archer Av for the AirTrain.

williamsburg.note: L to Broadway Junction, A to Howard Beach, AirTrain — or the slow one-seat J from Marcy Av to Sutphin; budget ~80 min either way, or a ~$55-75 car.

east-williamsburg.note: L to Broadway Junction, A to Howard Beach, then the AirTrain; tolerable off-peak, miserable with luggage at rush — a car is ~$55-70.

greenpoint.note: The worst of the ten: G to Court Sq, E to Jamaica, AirTrain — call it 90 minutes on a good day; most people just book a ~$60-80 car.

bushwick.note: Best-placed of the Brooklyn picks: L to Broadway Junction then A to Howard Beach, or J/Z along Broadway straight to Sutphin; ~$50-65 by car.

astoria.note: Awkward Queens-to-Queens trip: N/W to the 7 to the E at Roosevelt Av, then AirTrain — roughly 90 minutes, so most people pay ~$50-70 for a car.

long-island-city.note: Surprisingly clean: one E train from Court Sq to Sutphin Blvd-Archer Av, then the $8.75 AirTrain.

hells-kitchen.note: Fastest of the ten: LIRR from Moynihan/Penn to Jamaica in ~20 minutes plus AirTrain, or the cheaper one-seat E; a cab is $90-120 with the $70 flat fare, tolls, and fees.

yorkville.note: 4/5/6 to Grand Central, LIRR to Jamaica in ~20 minutes, then AirTrain — long but smooth; a cab runs $90-120 either direction.

east-village.note: F or M out to Jackson Heights-Roosevelt Av, then the free Q70 to the terminals; a car is ~$40-60.

lower-east-side.note: F from Delancey or East Broadway to Roosevelt Av, then the free Q70; workable, or a ~$40-60 car.

williamsburg.note: No good rail link from north Brooklyn: G to Court Sq, 7 to 74 St, free Q70 — most people pay $35-60 for a car instead.

east-williamsburg.note: A three-seat slog (L, G, 7, then the Q70) for a trip of about six miles; with a flight bag a ~$35-55 car wins.

greenpoint.note: LGA is only ~5 miles away but transit is a G-to-7-to-Q70 chain; a ~$30-45 car is hard to resist.

bushwick.note: The one-seat M loops through Manhattan before reaching Roosevelt Av for the Q70 — about 80 minutes for a 7-mile trip; cars run ~$40-55.

astoria.note: The easy one: the 24-hour M60 SBS runs down Astoria Blvd straight into the terminals — 25-40 minutes door to door, no reason for a cab.

long-island-city.note: 7 (or E/M from Court Sq) to 74 St-Roosevelt Av for the free Q70; ~45 minutes, or a ~$30-40 car.

hells-kitchen.note: E or 7 out to 74 St-Roosevelt Av, then the free Q70; cabs from Midtown run $50-75 with tolls and can crawl in traffic.

yorkville.note: 4/5/6 one stop to 125 St, then the M60 SBS over the RFK Bridge; 125th St traffic can drag, and a car is ~$35-50.

east-village.note: N/Q/R/W from Union Sq or 8 St run to 57 St-7 Av, then you walk in at 59 St; the 6 to 68 St-Hunter College covers the Fifth Ave entrances.

lower-east-side.note: The weekday B from Grand St lands at the 72 St-Central Park West entrance, no transfer; the D gets you Columbus Circle, and from the Essex end the F to 57 St leaves a walk.

williamsburg.note: No one-seat ride: L to Union Sq then a N/Q/R/W up Broadway, or M to 34 St then B/D up Central Park West — the park is a planned trip, not a stroll.

east-williamsburg.note: Same L-plus-Midtown-transfer as Williamsburg with ten more minutes on the front; for casual green space you'll use McCarren or Cooper Park instead.

greenpoint.note: G to Court Sq, E to 7 Av-53 St, then a walk up Seventh into the park — nothing about this trip is quick, so weekend visits take commitment.

bushwick.note: M up Sixth Ave then B/D to Central Park West, or L to Union Sq then a Broadway train — call it an hour to the lawn, the worst of the ten.

astoria.note: N/W run straight to 57 St-7 Av two blocks from the park's south edge — no transfer, then a ten-minute walk up to the lawn.

long-island-city.note: E or M from Court Sq to the 53 St stops then a walk up; the F from 21 St-Queensbridge reaches 57 St two blocks from the park. Short rides, walk-heavy endings.

hells-kitchen.note: From the upper 40s and 50s you can simply walk to Columbus Circle; otherwise the 1 to 66 St or any A/C/B/D to 59 St puts you at the park edge in minutes.

yorkville.note: Q one stop to 72 St then a crosstown walk to the Fifth Ave entrance — or skip the train entirely, since much of Yorkville can walk into the park along Museum Mile.

east-village.note: N/R/W to Herald Sq leaves a seven-minute walk with your bag; the L to 14 St with an A/C/E or 1/2/3 transfer drops you inside Penn instead.

lower-east-side.note: F or M direct to Herald Sq then a seven-minute walk; with luggage, F to W 4 St plus an A/C/E up to Penn keeps the whole trip underground.

williamsburg.note: M to Herald Sq plus a short walk is the default; from the Bedford end, the L to 14 St connects to the A/C/E, which lands inside the station.

east-williamsburg.note: L to 14 St then A/C/E or 1/2/3 into Penn; near the Flushing Av edge the M reaches Herald Sq direct with the walk left over.

greenpoint.note: G to Court Sq then the E runs straight into Penn — the transfer is unavoidable, but at least you surface inside the station.

bushwick.note: M to Herald Sq leaves a seven-minute walk; from the Wilson Av end budget closer to an hour, which stings when you're racing an Amtrak departure.

astoria.note: N/W direct to Herald Sq then a seven-minute walk to Penn — no transfer, just remember to pad the walk when you have a train to catch.

long-island-city.note: E from Court Sq surfaces inside Penn a few stops later — the easiest train-escape setup of the ten; the 7 to Times Sq is the backup.

hells-kitchen.note: Most of Hell's Kitchen walks to Penn in 10-15 minutes; with luggage it's one A/C/E stop from 42 St or the 1 from 50 St.

yorkville.note: Q direct to Herald Sq then a seven-minute walk; transferring to the 1/2/3 at Times Sq lands you inside Penn, worth it with a suitcase.
